title: Portable Context Control Plane — Request Signals Input
description: Schema for the requestSignals struct received by the orchestrator. Produced by the Request Router from the raw user request string — the router classifies the request and emits a structured signal set. It does NOT forward raw user text. Class A input — if requestSignals is absent entirely, the planning run halts. Three fields are required: promptFamily, familyConfidence, injectionSuspect. PRIVACY CRITICAL: This schema must NEVER include raw user request text, raw request content, or any field that contains user-supplied free-form text. Raw user text is untrusted and must not reach selectors in any form. The Request Router is the sole detection owner for injection signals. Selectors consume injectionSuspect as a precomputed boolean only — selector logic must never pattern-match raw request text. injectionSuspect is frozen as boolean for MVP (F-25 resolved). Future additive field injectionEvidenceCodes must NOT appear here. explicitCallerFlags are operator-supplied override flags only — never raw user text. Canonical owners: docs/06 §2.1–§2.2; docs/12 §3.3.
$comment: Pass 4.9C-5B — Batch B extension. Pass 4.9C-5B.1 — wording repair. Pass 4.9C-5B.2 — Re-entry Loops extensions: added reentryTurn, priorPlanId, loopSuspect as [FUTURE-ONLY] optional fields per docs/20_REENTRY_LOOPS_SCOPING.md §4.2, §4.3, §5.3. No code. No runtime. No provider/model calls. No docs edited. injectionSuspect is boolean only per F-25 resolved (Pass 4.7B Option A) — frozen MVP contract. promptFamily is a closed enum (PromptFamilyValue ref, 10 known values) — only known values are schema-valid at this boundary; JSON Schema does not perform substitution. Runtime handling of unknown promptFamily values (e.g., upstream Router normalization) is a separate design concern outside this schema's scope. familyConfidence is required — its absence silently disables the familyConfidence escalation rule (docs/06 §17.3.4) which is safety-relevant; missing field is schema-invalid. injectionEvidenceCodes is a future additive field — must NOT appear here and must NOT replace injectionSuspect boolean. Canonical sources: docs/06 §2.1–§2.2 §17; docs/12 §3.3; docs/20 §4.2–§4.3 §5.3.

promptFamily.title: Prompt family
promptFamily.description: The prompt family enum value for this planning run, produced by the Request Router. This is a closed enum — only the 10 canonical MVP values are schema-valid at this boundary. Values outside the known set are schema-invalid. JSON Schema does not perform substitution: it validates that the supplied value is one of the 10 known values and rejects anything else. Runtime handling of unknown prompt-family values (such as upstream Router normalization or orchestrator fallback) is a separate design concern and requires an explicit implementation decision — it is not described or implied by this schema. Canonical: docs/06 §2.2.

familyConfidence.title: Family confidence score
familyConfidence.description: Float 0.0–1.0. The Request Router's confidence in the promptFamily classification. Required because its value governs the familyConfidence escalation rule: when injectionSuspect: true and familyConfidence < selectorPolicy.failOpenThreshold, the effective injectionSuspectAction automatically escalates to fail_open_all (docs/06 §17.3.4). If this field is absent, the escalation rule cannot fire — making its absence a schema-invalid condition rather than a safe default. Low values trigger fail-open behavior in selectors: when below failOpenThreshold, selectors treat promptFamily as general_default and include more context. Canonical: docs/06 §2.1.

injectionSuspect.title: Injection suspect flag
injectionSuspect.description: Whether the Request Router detected adversarial injection patterns in the raw user request. Boolean only — strictly typed. This is the sole injection signal exposed to selectors in MVP (F-25 resolved, Pass 4.7B Option A — frozen boolean contract). Selectors consume this precomputed boolean only; they must never inspect raw request text or pattern codes independently. The Request Router is the sole detection owner. If injectionSuspect is present but not a boolean (e.g., a string or struct), the orchestrator treats it as false and emits injection_suspect_malformed — however, the schema rejects non-boolean values at the boundary. Future additive field injectionEvidenceCodes (for richer audit metadata) must NOT appear in this schema and must NOT replace or change the type of this field. Canonical: docs/06 §2.1; F-25.

activeSkillIds.title: Active skill IDs
activeSkillIds.description: Optional. IDs of skills currently active for this session, produced by the Request Router from validated request analysis. Not raw user text. If absent, selectors treat as empty array with no warning. If present but malformed, the orchestrator emits one selector-phase warning and treats as empty. Used by the skill selector for active-skill-match evidence atoms. Unknown IDs (not in componentsById) produce active_id_unknown planning warnings — distinct from reference_unknown SelectionDecision records. Canonical: docs/06 §2.1.

activeToolIds.title: Active tool IDs
activeToolIds.description: Optional. IDs of tools currently active for this session, produced by the Request Router. Not raw user text. If absent, selectors treat as empty array with no warning. Used by the tool selector for active-tool-match evidence atoms. Unknown IDs produce active_id_unknown planning warnings — distinct from reference_unknown. Canonical: docs/06 §2.1.

activeMemoryIds.title: Active memory IDs
activeMemoryIds.description: Optional. IDs of memory components currently active for this session, produced by the Request Router. Not raw user text. If absent, selectors treat as empty array with no warning. Used by the memory selector for active-memory-match evidence atoms. Unknown IDs produce active_id_unknown planning warnings — distinct from reference_unknown. Canonical: docs/06 §2.1.

outputFormatHint.title: Output format hint
outputFormatHint.description: Optional. The output format hint produced by the Request Router, matched by the output_format selector against component formatTag values (docs/05 §3). Open string — not a closed enum, because formatTag values are registry-defined and variable across deployments. null = no hint (default if absent). If absent, no match occurs and no warning is emitted. If present but malformed, orchestrator emits one warning and treats as null. Must not contain raw user text. Canonical: docs/06 §2.1.

explicitCallerFlags.title: Explicit caller flags
explicitCallerFlags.description: Optional. Operator-supplied override flags forwarded by the Request Router. These are operator-controlled configuration flags — they must NEVER contain raw user request text, user-supplied free-form strings, or any content derived from the untrusted user message. Selectors may read these flags for override logic. Examples: 'force_include_all_policy', 'debug_trace'. If absent, treated as empty array. Canonical: docs/06 §2.1.

reentryTurn.$comment: [FUTURE-ONLY] Re-entry Loops extension. Not active in MVP. Authority: docs/20_REENTRY_LOOPS_SCOPING.md §4.2.
reentryTurn.title: Re-entry turn flag
reentryTurn.description: [FUTURE-ONLY] Optional. Whether this planning call is a re-entry turn (i.e., the External Runtime is calling ZAM again after executing tool results). When true, signals the Request Router that promptFamily should be inherited from the prior turn unless reclassification is warranted, and signals Selectors that tool-use context (open_commitments lane) should be weighted higher. Absent or false = fresh turn (default MVP behavior). If absent, all MVP planning proceeds normally with no change. Must not be added to the required array. Canonical: docs/20 §4.2.

priorPlanId.$comment: [FUTURE-ONLY] Re-entry Loops extension. Not active in MVP. Authority: docs/20_REENTRY_LOOPS_SCOPING.md §4.3.
priorPlanId.title: Prior plan ID
priorPlanId.description: [FUTURE-ONLY] Optional. The runId of the prior planning turn's trace (trace.run.runId). Passed by the External Runtime on re-entry to link this planning call to the plan that triggered it. Enables reentryPhase[].priorPlanId in trace.json for auditable provenance. If absent, no prior-plan linkage is recorded. Must not be added to the required array. Canonical: docs/20 §4.3; trace.schema.json reentryPhase[].priorPlanId.

loopSuspect.$comment: [FUTURE-ONLY] Re-entry Loops extension. Not active in MVP. Authority: docs/20_REENTRY_LOOPS_SCOPING.md §5.3.
loopSuspect.title: Loop suspect flag
loopSuspect.description: [FUTURE-ONLY] Optional. Advisory signal from the Request Router indicating that the current re-entry request appears to be a degenerate loop (e.g., the history contains identical tool call/result pairs repeated beyond a threshold). When true, Selectors include a loop_suspect_seen evidence atom in their decisions. This signal does NOT cause ZAM to halt or refuse the request — it is an advisory trace signal for operator review only. The External Runtime is the loop enforcer; ZAM does not count iterations. If absent, treated as false (no loop suspect). Must not be added to the required array. Canonical: docs/20 §5.3.

examples.$comment: Baseline: known family, high confidence, no injection suspect
Injection suspect with warn_and_continue policy (effectiveInjectionSuspectAction determined by selector-policy)
Low familyConfidence — if < failOpenThreshold AND injectionSuspect: true, escalation to fail_open_all fires (docs/06 §17.3.4)
Full optional fields with output format hint
